title: Why is Vivado 2026.1 dropping Linux support for free tier?
summary: The discussion centers on AMD's decision to remove Linux support from the free tier of Vivado 2026.1, a hardware design tool for FPGAs. Users express frustration over reduced accessibility for hobbyists and open-source developers, citing vendor lock-in and shifting business strategies. The move is seen as a regression for the FPGA ecosystem.
